stmt: For every bosonic core `bc` and natural number `n`, `(bc.a ^ n) (bc.ket n) = ((n.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum`.
priority_rationale: Basic factorial evaluation on ket n; useful local ladder-operator formula.

stmt: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (m n : ℕ), n < m → (bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket n) = 0
priority_rationale: Basic vanishing law for over-annihilating a ket; useful local ladder formula.

stmt: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (s : Finset (ℕ × ℕ)) (c : ℕ × ℕ → ℂ), bc.number * (∑ p in s, c p • (bc.aDagger ^ p.1 * bc.a ^ p.2)) - (∑ p in s, c p • (bc.aDagger ^ p.1 * bc.a ^ p.2)) * bc.number = ∑ p in s, (((p.1 : ℂ) - (p.2 : ℂ)) * c p) • (bc.aDagger ^ p.1 * bc.a ^ p.2)
priority_rationale: Extends number commutator control to finite sums of mixed monomials.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `ℂ`-submodule `W ≤ V`, if `bc.vacuum ∈ W`, `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W`, and `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W`, then there exists `bcW : BosonCore W` such that for every `m n : ℕ` and every `w : W`, the underlying vector of `((bcW.aDagger ^ m * bcW.a ^ n) w)` is exactly `((bc.aDagger ^ m * bc.a ^ n) (w : V))`.
priority_rationale: Strengthens inherited-core existence with exact compatibility for all mixed monomials.

stmt: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (v : V) (μ : ℂ), bc.number v = μ • v → (∀ n : ℕ, (bc.aDagger ^ n) v ≠ 0) → LinearIndependent ℂ (fun n : ℕ => (bc.aDagger ^ n) v)
priority_rationale: Generalizes existing iterated-creation independence by removing the explicit vacuum hypothesis.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `ℂ`-submodule `W ≤ V`, if `bc.vacuum ∈ W`, `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W`, and `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W`, then there exists a unique bosonic core `bcW : BosonCore W` such that `(bcW.vacuum : V) = bc.vacuum`, `(bcW.a w : V) = bc.a (w : V)`, and `(bcW.aDagger w : V) = bc.aDagger (w : V)` for every `w : W`.
priority_rationale: Adds uniqueness to the already existing invariant-submodule boson-core construction.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `ℂ`-submodule `W ≤ V`, if `bc.vacuum ∈ W`, `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W`, and `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W`, and if `bcW : BosonCore W` satisfies `(bcW.vacuum : V) = bc.vacuum`, `(bcW.a w : V) = bc.a (w : V)`, and `(bcW.aDagger w : V) = bc.aDagger (w : V)` for every `w : W`, then with `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` there exists `hSW : S ≤ W` such that the image of `S` under `Submodule.inclusion hSW` is exactly `Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bcW.ket)`.
priority_rationale: Connects induced boson cores to the original Fock span functorially.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, then there exists an injective `ℂ`-linear map `Ψ : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) →ₗ[ℂ] Polynomial ℂ` such that `Ψ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = Polynomial.X ^ n` for every `n : ℕ`.
priority_rationale: Useful inverse-style coordinate map from the ket span into polynomials.

stmt: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (T : Module.End ℂ V), bc.number * (bc.aDagger * T) - (bc.aDagger * T) * bc.number = bc.aDagger * T + bc.aDagger * (bc.number * T - T * bc.number)
priority_rationale: Local commutator step for composing with aDagger on the left.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, vector `v : V`, scalar `μ : ℂ`, natural number `N`, and coefficient function `c : ℕ → ℂ`, if `bc.number v = μ • v`, then `bc.number (∑ k in Finset.range (N + 1), (c k : ℂ) • ((bc.aDagger ^ k) v)) = ∑ k in Finset.range (N + 1), ((((μ + (k : ℂ)) * c k) : ℂ)) • ((bc.aDagger ^ k) v)`.
priority_rationale: Useful finite-sum eigenvalue formula for created states.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, applying `bc.a` exactly `p.natDegree` times to `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum` yields `(((p.natDegree.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * p.coeff p.natDegree) • bc.vacuum`.
priority_rationale: Extracts the leading polynomial coefficient by repeated annihilation.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, the endomorphism obtained by evaluating `Polynomial.derivative p` at `bc.aDagger` is `∑ k in Finset.range (p.natDegree + 1), ((((k + 1 : ℕ) : ℂ) * p.coeff (k + 1)) : ℂ) • (bc.aDagger ^ k)`.
priority_rationale: Concrete derivative-evaluation expansion used by annihilation commutator formulas.

stmt: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (m : ℕ), m ∈ l.support → (∀ n : ℕ, n ∈ l.support → n ≤ m) → (bc.a ^ m) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = l m • ((bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket m))
priority_rationale: Cleaner top-support annihilation formula reducing to the single-ket case.

stmt: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (m : ℕ), (∀ n : ℕ, n ∈ l.support → n < m) → (bc.a ^ m) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = 0
priority_rationale: Useful vanishing criterion for annihilating combinations below a given level.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V` with `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, every nonzero `ℂ`-submodule `W` of `Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` that is invariant under the restricted annihilation operator contains the vacuum vector.
priority_rationale: Natural irreducibility lemma: annihilation-stable nonzero subspaces must contain the vacuum.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, let `Φ : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V` be defined by `Φ(p) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum`. Then `LinearMap.ker Φ` is an ideal of `Polynomial ℂ` that is stable under `Polynomial.derivative`; equivalently, if `Φ(p) = 0`, then `Φ(q * p) = 0` for every polynomial `q`, and `Φ(Polynomial.derivative p) = 0`.
priority_rationale: Structural kernel theorem for the polynomial realization; mainly useful in degenerate cases.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0` and `p ≠ 0`, then there exist a natural number `m` and a scalar `c : ℂ` with `c ≠ 0` such that `(bc.a ^ m) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = c • bc.vacuum`.
priority_rationale: Operational nonzero-to-vacuum reduction for polynomial states.

stmt: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * p) = bc.aDagger * Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p
priority_rationale: Simple but reusable multiplication-by-X compatibility for aeval at aDagger.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `ℂ`-submodule `W ≤ V`, if `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W` and `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W`, then the restricted endomorphisms `A, B : Module.End ℂ W` induced by `bc.a` and `bc.aDagger` satisfy `A * B - B * A = 1`.
priority_rationale: Basic restriction theorem showing CCR passes to invariant submodules.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `ℂ`-submodule `W ≤ V`, if `W ≠ ⊥`, `∀ v ∈ W, bc.a v ∈ W`, and `∀ v ∈ W, bc.aDagger v ∈ W`, then `W` is not finite-dimensional over `ℂ`.
priority_rationale: Extends infinite-dimensionality from vacuum-containing invariant submodules to arbitrary nonzero ones.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, scalar `μ : ℂ`, and nonzero finitely supported coefficient function `l : ℕ →₀ ℂ`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0` and `bc.number (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = μ • Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l`, then there exists a unique natural number `n` such that `l n ≠ 0`.
priority_rationale: Sharp support-level form of eigenvector concentration in the ket basis.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, the endomorphism obtained by evaluating `p` at `bc.aDagger` is the finite sum `∑ n in Finset.range (p.natDegree + 1), (p.coeff n : ℂ) • (bc.aDagger ^ n)`.
priority_rationale: Foundational expansion of polynomial evaluation into powers of aDagger.

stmt: For every bosonic core `bc`, vector `v`, scalar `μ : ℂ`, and polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, if `bc.number v = μ • v`, then `bc.number ((aeval bc.aDagger) p v) = ((aeval bc.aDagger) (Polynomial.comp (Polynomial.X + Polynomial.C μ) p) v)`.
priority_rationale: Useful polynomial-calculus reformulation of number action on eigenvectors.

stmt: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (m : ℕ), m ∈ l.support → (∀ n : ℕ, n ∈ l.support → n ≤ m) → (bc.a ^ m) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = ((((m.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * l m) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum
priority_rationale: Operational lemma extracting the top Fock coefficient by repeated annihilation.

stmt: For every complex vector space V, bosonic core bc : BosonCore V, and finitely supported coefficient function l : ℕ →₀ ℂ, bc.number (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = ∑ n in l.support, (((n : ℂ) * l n) : ℂ) • bc.ket n.
priority_rationale: Diagonal action of number on ket combinations is a useful local normal form.

stmt: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (μ : ℂ) {v : V} (m n : ℕ), bc.number v = μ • v → bc.number ((bc.aDagger ^ m * bc.a ^ n) v) = (μ + (m : ℂ) - (n : ℂ)) • ((bc.aDagger ^ m * bc.a ^ n) v)
priority_rationale: Reusable eigenvalue-shift formula for mixed creation-annihilation monomials.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, natural number `N`, and coefficient function `c : ℕ → ℂ`, if `T = ∑ k in Finset.range (N + 1), (c k : ℂ) • (bc.aDagger ^ k)`, then `bc.number * T - T * bc.number = ∑ k in Finset.range (N + 1), (((k : ℂ) * c k) : ℂ) • (bc.aDagger ^ k)`.
priority_rationale: Finite-sum number commutator formula supporting polynomial operator calculus.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, scalar `μ : ℂ`, and finitely supported coefficient function `l : ℕ →₀ ℂ`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0` and `bc.number (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = μ • Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l`, then for every `n : ℕ`, `l n ≠ 0` implies `μ = (n : ℂ)`.
priority_rationale: Natural coefficientwise consequence of ket-basis eigenvector equations.

stmt: ∀ (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)) bc.vacuum
priority_rationale: Vacuum version of annihilation-as-derivative on polynomial states; useful bridge lemma.

stmt: ∀ (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ) (v : V), bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) v) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) (bc.a v) + (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)) v
priority_rationale: General operator-level Leibniz rule for annihilation against polynomial creation operators.

stmt: ∀ (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum = ∑ n in Finset.range (p.natDegree + 1), (p.coeff n : ℂ) • bc.ket n
priority_rationale: Concrete finite-sum expression for polynomial states in the ket basis.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V` with `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, there exists a `ℂ`-linear map `Φ : Polynomial ℂ → Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` such that `Φ 1` is `bc.vacuum` viewed in the ket span, `Φ (Polynomial.X ^ n)` is `bc.ket n` viewed in the ket span for every `n : ℕ`, and for every polynomial `p`, `Φ (Polynomial.X * p)` is the restricted creation operator applied to `Φ p` while `Φ (Polynomial.derivative p)` is the restricted annihilation operator applied to `Φ p`.
priority_rationale: Natural polynomial realization of the ket span, but weaker than the full equivalence form.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, natural number `N`, and coefficient function `c : ℕ → ℂ`, if `T = ∑ k in Finset.range (N + 1), (c k : ℂ) • (bc.aDagger ^ k)`, then `bc.a * T - T * bc.a = ∑ k in Finset.range N, ((((k + 1 : ℕ) : ℂ) * c (k + 1)) : ℂ) • (bc.aDagger ^ k)`.
priority_rationale: Concrete finite-sum commutator formula supporting the polynomial commutator cluster.

stmt: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Φ : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)`, if `Φ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)` for every `n : ℕ`, then for every `l : ℕ →₀ ℂ`, the image under `Φ` of `bc.number (Φ.symm l)` viewed in the ket span is `l.sum (fun n c => Finsupp.single n ((n : ℂ) * c))`.

For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Φ : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)`, if `Φ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)` for every `n : ℕ`, then for every `l : ℕ →₀ ℂ`, the underlying vector of `Φ.symm l` is exactly `Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l`.

∀ (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = ∑ n in Finset.range (p.natDegree + 1), ((((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℂ) * p.coeff (n + 1)) : ℂ) • bc.ket n

For every complex vector space V, bosonic core bc : BosonCore V, and finitely supported coefficient function l : ℕ →₀ ℂ, bc.a (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = ∑ n in l.support.erase 0, (((n : ℂ) * l n) : ℂ) • bc.ket (n - 1).

For every complex vector space V, bosonic core bc : BosonCore V, and finitely supported coefficient function l : ℕ →₀ ℂ, if bc.vacuum ≠ 0 and bc.a (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = 0, then l.support ⊆ {0}.

For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, let `Phi : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V` be defined by `Phi(p) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum`, and let `Q := Polynomial ℂ ⧸ LinearMap.ker Phi`. Then there exist `ℂ`-linear endomorphisms `A, A† : Module.End ℂ Q`, induced respectively by `Polynomial.derivative` and multiplication by `Polynomial.X`, such that `A * A† - A† * A = 1`, `A` annihilates the class of `1`, and there exists a `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Ψ : Q ≃ₗ[ℂ] Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` that intertwines `A` and `A†` with the restrictions of `bc.a` and `bc.aDagger` to `Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`.

For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, let `Phi : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V` be defined by `Phi(p) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum`. Then `LinearMap.ker Phi = ⊥` if and only if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, and `LinearMap.ker Phi = ⊤` if and only if `bc.vacuum = 0`.

∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = l.sum (fun n c => (p.eval (n : ℂ) * c) • bc.ket n)

For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, for every finitely supported function `l : ℕ →₀ ℂ`, one has
`bc.aDagger (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) (l.sum fun n c => Finsupp.single (n + 1) c)`.

Let `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`. For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Φ : S ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)` satisfying `Φ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)` for every `n : ℕ`, the restricted ladder operators satisfy the basis formulas
`Φ ⟨bc.aDagger (bc.ket n), by simp [BosonCore.aDagger_ket, S]⟩ = Finsupp.single (n + 1) (1 : ℂ)`,
`Φ ⟨bc.a (bc.ket 0), by simp [BosonCore.ket_zero, bc.vacuum_annihilate, S]⟩ = 0`,
and
`Φ ⟨bc.a (bc.ket (n + 1)), by simp [BosonCore.a_ket_succ, S]⟩ = ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℂ) • Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)` for every `n : ℕ`.

For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, submodule `W : Submodule ℂ V`, and vector `w : V`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, `w ∈ W`, `w ≠ 0`, `w ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`, and `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W`, then `bc.vacuum ∈ W`.

For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, submodule `W : Submodule ℂ V`, and polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum ∈ W`, `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, and `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W`, then `bc.vacuum ∈ W`.

∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (v : V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) v) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) (bc.number v) + (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative p)) v

For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V` with `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, let `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`. Then there exists a `ℂ`-linear endomorphism `N_S : Module.End ℂ S` such that for every `s : S`, the underlying vector of `N_S s` is exactly `bc.number (s : V)`, and for every `n : ℕ`, `N_S ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = (n : ℂ) • ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩`.

For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V` with `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, let `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`. Then there exists a `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Φ : S ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)` such that `Φ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)` for every `n : ℕ`, and for every polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, the image under `Φ` of the vector `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum` viewed in `S` is exactly `p.toFinsupp`.

For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V` with `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, there exists a `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Φ : Polynomial ℂ ≃ₗ[ℂ] Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` such that for every polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, the underlying vector of `Φ p` is exactly `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum`.

For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, let `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`. For every `A : Module.End ℂ S`, if `(A s : V) = bc.a (s : V)` for every `s : S`, then `A ⟨bc.ket 0, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self 0)⟩ = 0` and for every `n : ℕ`, `A ⟨bc.ket (n + 1), Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self (n + 1))⟩ = ((n + 1 : ℂ)) • ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩`.

For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0` and `bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = 0`, then there exists `c : ℂ` such that `p = Polynomial.C c`.

∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (n : ℕ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → n ∈ l.support → ∃ p : Polynomial ℂ, (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun m : ℕ => bc.ket m) l) = l n • bc.ket n

∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (q : Polynomial ℂ) (n : ℕ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → n ∈ q.support → ∃ p : Polynomial ℂ, (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum) = q.coeff n • bc.ket n

∀ (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), (∀ s : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket), bc.aDagger (s : V) ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)) ∧ (∀ s : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket), bc.a (s : V) ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket))

∀ (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), ∃ A AD : Module.End ℂ (Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)), (∀ s : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket), ((A s : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)) : V) = bc.a (s : V)) ∧ (∀ s : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket), ((AD s : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)) : V) = bc.aDagger (s : V))
∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (n : ℕ) (z : ℂ), bc.a * (z • (bc.aDagger ^ (n + 1))) - (z • (bc.aDagger ^ (n + 1))) * bc.a = (((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℂ) * z) • (bc.aDagger ^ n)
∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (s : Finset ℕ) (f : ℕ → Module.End ℂ V), bc.a * (∑ k in s, f k) - (∑ k in s, f k) * bc.a = ∑ k in s, (bc.a * f k - f k * bc.a)

∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (T : Module.End ℂ V) (n : ℕ), bc.a * (bc.aDagger ^ (n + 1) * T) - (bc.aDagger ^ (n + 1) * T) * bc.a = ((n + 1 : ℂ) • (bc.aDagger ^ n * T)) + bc.aDagger ^ (n + 1) * (bc.a * T - T * bc.a)

∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (T : Module.End ℂ V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.a * (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p * T) - (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p * T) * bc.a = Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p) * T + Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p * (bc.a * T - T * bc.a)

For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, vector `v : V`, and natural number `n`, if `bc.number v = (n : ℂ) • v`, `v ≠ 0`, and `(bc.a ^ (n + 1)) v = 0`, then `(bc.a ^ n) v ≠ 0`.

For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, vector `v : V`, and scalar `μ : ℂ`, if `bc.number v = μ • v`, `v ≠ 0`, and there exists a natural number `m` such that `(bc.a ^ m) v = 0`, then there exists a unique natural number `n` such that `μ = (n : ℂ)`, `(bc.a ^ (n + 1)) v = 0`, and `(bc.a ^ n) v ≠ 0`.
